name: Beef Pita, Greek Style
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large frying pan, combine ground beef, onion, and garlic. Cook, stirring frequently until the beef loses its pink color. Remove from heat.
2. Pour off excess drippings. Add mushrooms, bay leaf, salt, chili powder, cumin powder, cinnamon, tomato sauce, and wine. Return to heat; cook, stirring frequently, for about 5 minutes or until mushrooms are tender.
3. Stir in the remaining ingredients (tomato sauce, cheese, feta) and blend well. Set aside while preparing the pastry filling.
4. In a medium bowl, combine egg, creamed cottage cheese, crumbled feta, and cinnamon. Beat with an electric mixer until smooth.
5. Brush melted butter on a 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
6. Line the pan with one sheet of phyllo pastry, fitting it to the contours of the pan. Brush with more butter.
7. Layer with three additional sheets of phyllo pastry, brushing each with butter.
8. Sprinkle bread crumbs evenly over the top.
9. Spoon about 1/5 of the meat filling on top of the crumbs and about 1/5 of the cheese filling on top of the meat.
10. Place one sheet of phyllo pastry over the cheese filling, crinkling it to fit inside the pan's dimensions. Brush with butter and layer with another portion of meat and cheese fillings.
11. Repeat with three more sheets of phyllo pastry, crinkling each and brushing with butter before topping with additional fillings.
12. Fold in the bottom pastry edges over the filling. Smooth remaining 8 sheets of phyllo pastry over top, brushing each with butter.
13. Using a spatula, tuck the top pastry sheets around the inside edges of the pan. Score the top lightly in half lengthwise and sixths crosswise (do not cut through).
14. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 1 hour or until golden brown. Allow to cool at least 10 minutes before cutting along scored lines.
15. Arrange cherry tomatoes on each of 12 small wooden picks, inserting the pick into the center of each serving.
16. Garnish individual servings with parsley and fresh fruit kabobs, if desired.
FRESH FRUIT KABOBS:
1. To make fresh fruit kabobs, place chunks of fresh pineapple, cantaloupe, whole strawberries or other seasonal fruits on small wooden skewers.
